body.title: [design] Runtime self-healing test automation via SWADL
body.description:
Build a test harness that detects narrow 'this thing no longer exists' failures at runtime and escalates to CC to rewrite the code in place — the system heals itself without human intervention. SWADL is the execution harness; CC is the repair agent. Scope is narrow: reference failures (missing function, moved file, renamed symbol) not logic bugs. Akien and CC design together in one session.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in design session
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** In scope: detection of structural reference failures, escalation to CC, in-place code rewrite, re-run to confirm. Out of scope: logic bugs, behavioral regressions, anything requiring human judgment on correctness.
**Completion criteria:** A test that references a deliberately deleted symbol detects the failure, escalates, CC rewrites the reference, test passes on re-run — all without human intervention.
